From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Genus of plants native to Australia

| *Xylomelum* | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| *[Xylomelum pyriforme](/wiki/Xylomelum_pyriforme "Xylomelum pyriforme")* | |
| [Scientific classification](/wiki/Taxonomy_(biology) "Taxonomy (biology)") [Edit this classification](/wiki/Template:Taxonomy/Xylomelum "Edit this classification") | |
| Kingdom: | [Plantae](/wiki/Plant "Plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Tracheophytes](/wiki/Vascular_plant "Vascular plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Angiosperms](/wiki/Flowering_plant "Flowering plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Eudicots](/wiki/Eudicots "Eudicots") |
| Order: | [Proteales](/wiki/Proteales "Proteales") |
| Family: | [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") |
| Subfamily: | [Grevilleoideae](/wiki/Grevilleoideae "Grevilleoideae") |
| Tribe: | [Roupaleae](/wiki/Roupaleae "Roupaleae") |
| Subtribe: | [Lambertiinae](/wiki/Lambertiinae "Lambertiinae") |
| Genus: | *Xylomelum* [Sm.](/wiki/James_Edward_Smith_(botanist) "James Edward Smith (botanist)")[[1]](#cite_note-APC-1) |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/CapturedeFructibus.jpg/250px-CapturedeFructibus.jpg)](/wiki/File:CapturedeFructibus.jpg)

Engraving of *[Banksia pyriformis](/wiki/Xylomelum_pyriforme "Xylomelum pyriforme")* (fruit and seed) from Gaertner's 'De Fructibus...'

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/aa/X_pyriforme_flowers_Stotts.jpg/250px-X_pyriforme_flowers_Stotts.jpg)](/wiki/File:X_pyriforme_flowers_Stotts.jpg)

*[X. pyriforme](/wiki/Xylomelum_pyriforme "Xylomelum pyriforme")* flowers

***Xylomelum*** is a [genus](/wiki/Genus "Genus") of six species of flowering plants, often commonly known as **woody pears**, in the family [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") and are [endemic](/wiki/Endemism "Endemism") to Australia. Plants in this genus are tall shrubs or small trees with leaves arranged in opposite pairs, relatively small flowers arranged in spike-like groups, and the fruit a woody, more or less pear-shaped [follicle](/wiki/Follicle_(fruit) "Follicle (fruit)").

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Xylomelum&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Description")]

Plants in the genus *Xylomelum* are shrubs or trees that typically grow to a height of 4–14 m (13–46 ft) and have simple, leathery leaves arranged in opposite pairs. Juvenile leaves have coarse, sometimes prickly teeth on the edges. The flowers are arranged in dense, spike-like [racemes](/wiki/Raceme "Raceme") or [panicles](/wiki/Panicle "Panicle"), the flowers in pairs with a [bract](/wiki/Bract "Bract") at the base. Each flower has four similar [tepals](/wiki/Tepal "Tepal") that roll back as the flower develops revealing four [stamens](/wiki/Stamen "Stamen"). The fruit is a woody, more or less pear-shaped follicle attached at the larger end, that eventually splits into two halves and releases two winged seeds.[[2]](#cite_note-ABRS-2)[[3]](#cite_note-RBGS-3)[[4]](#cite_note-FloraBase-4)

## Taxonomy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Xylomelum&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Taxonomy")]

The genus *Xylomelum* was first formally described in 1798 by [James Edward Smith](/wiki/James_Edward_Smith_(botanist) "James Edward Smith (botanist)") in *[Transactions of the Linnean Society of London](/wiki/Linnean_Society_of_London#Publications "Linnean Society of London")*.[[5]](#cite_note-APNI-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Smith_1798-6) The name *Xylomelum* is derived from the Greek *xylon* meaning "wood" and *melon* "tree-fruit", referring to the woody fruit.[[2]](#cite_note-ABRS-2)

In 1979, [Farr](/w/index.php?title=Ellen_R._Farr&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ellen R. Farr (page does not exist)"), [Leussink](/w/index.php?title=Jan_A._Leussink&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jan A. Leussink (page does not exist)") and [Stafleu](/wiki/Frans_Antonie_Stafleu "Frans Antonie Stafleu") nominated *Xylomelum pyriforme* (Gaertn.) R.Br. as the [lectotype](/wiki/Type_(biology)#Lectotype "Type (biology)"), later corrected to *Xylomelum pyriforme* (Gaert.) Knight.[[7]](#cite_note-APNI1-7)

### Evolution

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Xylomelum&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Evolution")]

The "pears" of *Xylomelum* species are thought to have evolved primarily as a defense against [seed predation](/wiki/Seed_predation "Seed predation"). However, these woody fruits also provide protection of the seeds from fire. Johnson and Briggs (1963) consider the woody seeds in [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") to be a late evolutionary adaptation to fire.[[8]](#cite_note-JohnsonBriggs-8)

### Species list

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Xylomelum&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Species list")]

The following is a list of species accepted by the [Australian Plant Census](/wiki/Australian_Plant_Census "Australian Plant Census") as at November 2020:[[9]](#cite_note-APC1-9)

* *[Xylomelum angustifolium](/wiki/Xylomelum_angustifolium "Xylomelum angustifolium")* [Meisn.](/wiki/Meisn. "Meisn.") (W.A.)
* *[Xylomelum benthamii](/wiki/Xylomelum_benthamii "Xylomelum benthamii")* [Orchard](/wiki/Anthony_Edward_Orchard "Anthony Edward Orchard") (Qld.)
* *[Xylomelum cunninghamianum](/wiki/Xylomelum_cunninghamianum "Xylomelum cunninghamianum")* [Foreman](/wiki/Donald_Bruce_Foreman "Donald Bruce Foreman") (Qld., N.S.W.)
* *[Xylomelum occidentale](/wiki/Xylomelum_occidentale "Xylomelum occidentale")* [R.Br.](/wiki/R.Br. "R.Br.") (W.A.)
* *[Xylomelum pyriforme](/wiki/Xylomelum_pyriforme "Xylomelum pyriforme")* ([Gaertn.](/wiki/Joseph_Gaertner "Joseph Gaertner")) [Knight](/wiki/Joseph_Knight_(horticulturist) "Joseph Knight (horticulturist)") (N.S.W.)
* *[Xylomelum scottianum](/wiki/Xylomelum_scottianum "Xylomelum scottianum")* ([F.Muell.](/wiki/F.Muell. "F.Muell.")) [F.Muell.](/wiki/F.Muell. "F.Muell.") (Qld.)

A seventh species, *Xylomelum salicinum* [A.Cunn.](/wiki/A.Cunn. "A.Cunn.") ex R.Br. is listed at [Plants of the World Online](/wiki/Plants_of_the_World_Online "Plants of the World Online")[[10]](#cite_note-POWO-10) but is regarded as a [synonym](/wiki/Synonym_(taxonomy) "Synonym (taxonomy)") of *[X. scottianum](/wiki/Xylomelum_scottianum "Xylomelum scottianum")* by the Australian Plant Census.[[11]](#cite_note-APC2-11) In 2007, [Anthony Edward Orchard](/wiki/Anthony_Edward_Orchard "Anthony Edward Orchard") suggested that *X. salicinum* (Meisn.) Benth. is a [superfluous, illegitimate name](/wiki/Nomen_illegitimum "Nomen illegitimum") and raised *X. pyriforme* var. *salicinum* Meisn. to species status as *[X. benthamii](/wiki/Xylomelum_benthamii "Xylomelum benthamii")*.[[12]](#cite_note-Orchard-12)

In 1788, [Joseph Gaertner](/wiki/Joseph_Gaertner "Joseph Gaertner") described *Banksia pyriformis* in *[De Fructibus et Seminibus Plantarum](/wiki/De_Fructibus_et_Seminibus_Plantarum "De Fructibus et Seminibus Plantarum")*,[[13]](#cite_note-APNI2-13)[[14]](#cite_note-Gaertn.-14) a species whose genus was effectively reclassified when Smith described the genus, *Xylomelum* in 1798. Smith gave the essential characters of *Xylomelum* as: flower spikes with simple scales; flowers with four petals bearing four stamens, with a blunt, club-shaped stigma, with a uni-locular capsule having two seeds, the seeds being winged.[[6]](#cite_note-Smith_1798-6)

In 1810, [Robert Brown](/wiki/Robert_Brown_(botanist,_born_1773) "Robert Brown (botanist, born 1773)") gave a key to the genera of [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") in *[Transactions of the Linnean Society of London](/wiki/Linnean_Society_of_London#Publications "Linnean Society of London")*, placing *Xylomelum* in the group of [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") which have [dehiscent](/wiki/Dehiscent "Dehiscent") fruit and are [unilocular](/wiki/Unilocular "Unilocular") with two seeds, thereby grouping *[Anadenia](/wiki/Grevillea "Grevillea")*, *[Grevillea](/wiki/Grevillea "Grevillea")*, *[Hakea](/wiki/Hakea "Hakea")*, *[Lambertia](/wiki/Lambertia "Lambertia")*, *[Orites](/wiki/Orites "Orites")* and *[Rhopala](/wiki/Roupala "Roupala")* together. Within the group, *Xylomelum* is distinguished from *[Orites](/wiki/Orites "Orites")* and *[Rhopala](/wiki/Roupala "Roupala")* by having winged seeds, a thickened woody follicle with an excentric [locule](/wiki/Locule "Locule"), and a club-shaped [stigma](/wiki/Stigma_(botany) "Stigma (botany)").[[15]](#cite_note-APNI3-15)[[16]](#cite_note-Brown_Proteaceae_1811-16)

In 1888, [Engler](/wiki/Adolf_Engler "Adolf Engler"), in *[Die Natürlichen Pflanzenfamilien](/wiki/Die_Nat%C3%BCrlichen_Pflanzenfamilien "Die Natürlichen Pflanzenfamilien")*, divided the [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae") into two sub-families, [Persoonioideae](/wiki/Persoonioideae "Persoonioideae") and [Grevilloideae](/wiki/Grevilleoideae "Grevilleoideae"), placing *Xylomelum* in [Grevilloideae](/wiki/Grevilleoideae "Grevilleoideae"), and as being like *[Helicia](/wiki/Helicia "Helicia")* in having the flower axis at the base with four glands, but differing from it by having the inflorescence in 'ears', not in loose 'grapes', and by having seeds with wings.[[17]](#cite_note-EnglerProt-17)

In 1975, [Johnson](/wiki/L.A.S.Johnson "L.A.S.Johnson") and [Briggs](/wiki/Barbara_G._Briggs "Barbara G. Briggs") placed *Xylomelum* in the subfamily [Grevilleoideae](/wiki/Grevilleoideae "Grevilleoideae") and the tribe [Heliciaea](/w/index.php?title=Heliciaea&action=edit&redlink=1 "Heliciaea (page does not exist)") (together with *[Helicia](/wiki/Helicia "Helicia")*, *[Triunia](/wiki/Triunia "Triunia")* and *[Hollandaea](/wiki/Hollandaea "Hollandaea")*).[[18]](#cite_note-18) However, [Weston](/w/index.php?title=Peter_H._Weston&action=edit&redlink=1 "Peter H. Weston (page does not exist)") and [Barker](/w/index.php?title=Nigel_P._Barker&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nigel P. Barker (page does not exist)") (2006),[[19]](#cite_note-Weston_2006-19) argue that within the tribe of [Roupaleae](/wiki/Roupaleae "Roupaleae"), *Xylomelum* should be placed in the subtribe of [Lambertiinae](/wiki/Lambertiinae "Lambertiinae") and not together with the *[Helicia](/wiki/Helicia "Helicia")* in the subtribe of [Heliciinae](/wiki/Heliciinae "Heliciinae"),[[19]](#cite_note-Weston_2006-19) using in part, the evidence of the molecular studies of [Hoot](/w/index.php?title=Sara_B._Hoot&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sara B. Hoot (page does not exist)") and [Douglas](/w/index.php?title=A.W._Douglas&action=edit&redlink=1 "A.W. Douglas (page does not exist)"),[[20]](#cite_note-Hoot1998-20) who pair *Xylomelum* with *[Lambertia](/wiki/Lambertia "Lambertia")*.[[20]](#cite_note-Hoot1998-20)

See [Grevilleoideae](/wiki/Grevilleoideae "Grevilleoideae") for further discussion of the placement of *Xylomelum* within the [Proteaceae](/wiki/Proteaceae "Proteaceae").

## Distribution and habitat

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Xylomelum&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Distribution and habitat")]

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4a/Commons-logo.svg/40px-Commons-logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Commons-logo.svg)

Wikimedia Commons has media related to [Xylomelum](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:Xylomelum "commons:Category:Xylomelum").

Two species, *[X. angustifolium](/wiki/Xylomelum_angustifolium "Xylomelum angustifolium")* and *[X. occidentale](/wiki/Xylomelum_occidentale "Xylomelum occidentale")*, are endemic to Western Australia[[21]](#cite_note-FloraBase2-21)[[22]](#cite_note-FloraBase3-22) and the remaining five species are found in New South Wales and Queensland.[[1]](#cite_note-APC-1) All are either [mesophytes](/wiki/Mesophyte "Mesophyte") or grow in dry environments.[[4]](#cite_note-FloraBase-4)
